questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A city council is considering a rule that would require all council meetings to be announced at least one week in advance. The rule would also require meeting agendas to be posted online before the meeting begins. Supporters argue that residents cannot participate meaningfully if they do not know what officials plan to discuss.
Which civic principle is most directly supported by this proposal?

choices.A: Judicial review
choices.B: Transparency in government
choices.C: Federal supremacy
choices.D: Separation of powers

questions.correctAnswerText: Transparency in government
questions.explanation: Posting meeting notices and agendas helps residents see and understand government activity.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A civics textbook explains that the Framers divided federal authority among three branches. Congress makes laws, the president carries out laws, and federal courts interpret laws. The same textbook notes that each branch has ways to limit the others.
Which statement best explains why this system was created?

choices.A: To allow state governments to control all federal decisions
choices.B: To make sure the president can act without delay
choices.C: To prevent any one branch from gaining too much power
choices.D: To remove courts from political disputes

questions.correctAnswerText: To prevent any one branch from gaining too much power
questions.explanation: Separation of powers and checks and balances are designed to limit concentrated power.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
During the 1760s and 1770s, many colonists objected to British taxes passed after the French and Indian War. They argued that Parliament had no right to tax them because the colonies did not elect representatives to Parliament. British officials answered that the taxes helped pay for defending the colonies.
Which issue was at the center of this disagreement?

choices.A: Whether colonists should be allowed to move west
choices.B: Whether taxation required political representation
choices.C: Whether Britain should end trade with the colonies
choices.D: Whether the colonies should create a national bank

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether taxation required political representation
questions.explanation: The source describes colonists objecting to taxes because they lacked elected representatives in Parliament.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A town tracked the average price of a basic grocery basket.
Year: 2021, 2022, 2023
Average price: $80, $88, $96
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Prices decreased each year.
choices.B: The grocery basket became more expensive over time.
choices.C: The price stayed the same from 2021 to 2023.
choices.D: The grocery basket cost twice as much in 2023 as in 2021.

questions.correctAnswerText: The grocery basket became more expensive over time.
questions.explanation: The average price increased from $80 to $88 to $96.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A school district holds a public meeting before changing its student transportation policy. Residents may speak for two minutes, submit written comments, or email the board before the vote.
Which TWO actions are examples of civic participation in this situation?

choices.A: Speaking during the public comment period
choices.B: Waiting for officials to decide without giving input
choices.C: Emailing the school board about the policy
choices.D: Ignoring the issue because it affects only students
choices.E: Refusing to follow the policy after it passes

questions.correctAnswerText: Speaking during the public comment period | Emailing the school board about the policy
questions.explanation: Both actions involve residents communicating with public officials about a policy decision.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A coastal region has experienced stronger storms and repeated flooding. Local officials are considering building higher sea walls, restoring wetlands, and limiting construction in the most flood-prone areas.
Which geographic concept is most clearly shown in this scenario?

choices.A: Human-environment interaction
choices.B: Cultural diffusion
choices.C: Absolute monarchy
choices.D: Specialization of labor

questions.correctAnswerText: Human-environment interaction
questions.explanation: The scenario shows people responding to environmental risks such as flooding and storms.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
The First Amendment protects freedom of speech, religion, press, assembly, and petition. These protections limit the ability of government to punish people for expressing views or practicing religion peacefully.
Which conclusion is best supported by this information?

choices.A: Individual liberties are protected from certain government actions.
choices.B: Citizens may ignore laws they disagree with.
choices.C: State governments may cancel federal rights.
choices.D: The president may decide which rights apply.

questions.correctAnswerText: Individual liberties are protected from certain government actions.
questions.explanation: The First Amendment protects several freedoms by limiting government power over expression and religion.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A city’s unemployment rate was 5% last year and 7% this year. By how many percentage points did the unemployment rate increase?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: The unemployment rate increased from 5% to 7%, a change of 2 percentage points.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
In the early 1800s, reformers campaigned for public schools, prison reform, and better treatment of people with mental illness. Many reformers argued that society could be improved through education, moral action, and changes to public institutions.
Which broader idea best connects these reform movements?

choices.A: Citizens and governments could work to improve society.
choices.B: Westward expansion should be stopped immediately.
choices.C: Political parties should be eliminated.
choices.D: Industrial production should be replaced by farming.

questions.correctAnswerText: Citizens and governments could work to improve society.
questions.explanation: The source connects reform movements through the belief that institutions and society could be improved.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: The power to declare war belongs to [D1], while the power to command the armed forces belongs to [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=Congress; D2=the president
questions.explanation: Congress declares war, while the president serves as commander in chief.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A county election office reported the following turnout.
Age group: 18–29, 30–44, 45–64, 65+
Eligible voters: 20,000, 25,000, 30,000, 15,000
Voters who cast ballots: 8,000, 12,500, 18,000, 10,500
Which age group had the highest voter turnout rate?
questions.explanation: Turnout rates are 40%, 50%, 60%, and 70%; the 65+ group has the highest rate.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A region recorded the following population changes.
Year: 2000, 2010, 2020
Urban population: 1.2 million, 1.7 million, 2.4 million
Rural population: 2.0 million, 1.8 million, 1.5 million
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The rural population grew faster than the urban population.
choices.B: The urban population increased while the rural population decreased.
choices.C: The total population remained unchanged.
choices.D: Urban and rural populations were equal in 2020.

questions.correctAnswerText: The urban population increased while the rural population decreased.
questions.explanation: Urban population rose from 1.2 million to 2.4 million, while rural population fell from 2.0 million to 1.5 million.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A state law conflicts with a federal law on the same issue. A judge rules that the federal law controls because the Constitution and federal laws made under it are the “supreme Law of the Land.”
Which constitutional principle is the judge applying?

choices.A: Popular sovereignty
choices.B: Federal supremacy
choices.C: Due process
choices.D: Judicial restraint

questions.correctAnswerText: Federal supremacy
questions.explanation: The Supremacy Clause gives valid federal law priority over conflicting state law.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
After the Civil War, the Reconstruction Amendments abolished slavery, defined national citizenship, promised equal protection under the law, and prohibited denying the vote based on race. These amendments changed the relationship between the federal government and the states.
Which statement best describes one effect of the Reconstruction Amendments?

choices.A: They expanded federal protection of civil rights.
choices.B: They ended all political conflict in the South.
choices.C: They gave states complete control over citizenship.
choices.D: They created the first national income tax.

questions.correctAnswerText: They expanded federal protection of civil rights.
questions.explanation: The Reconstruction Amendments addressed slavery, citizenship, equal protection, and voting rights.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A factory owner can use a limited budget to buy either a new machine or additional advertising. The machine would increase production, while advertising might increase sales. The owner chooses the machine.
What is the opportunity cost of this decision?

choices.A: The money already spent on the factory building
choices.B: The additional advertising the owner gave up
choices.C: The total number of workers at the factory
choices.D: The price customers pay for the product

questions.correctAnswerText: The additional advertising the owner gave up
questions.explanation: Opportunity cost is the next-best alternative that is not chosen.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
During the late 1800s, many Americans moved to cities as factories expanded. Cities offered jobs, transportation networks, and access to markets, but they also faced crowding, pollution, and housing shortages.
Which TWO developments were effects of industrialization and urban growth?

choices.A: Growth of wage labor in factories
choices.B: Immediate disappearance of poverty in cities
choices.C: Expansion of urban housing and transportation needs
choices.D: Elimination of immigration to the United States

questions.correctAnswerText: Growth of wage labor in factories | Expansion of urban housing and transportation needs
questions.explanation: Industrialization increased factory work and created new urban infrastructure needs.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A person accused of a crime must be informed of the charges, have the chance to hear evidence, and be allowed to respond before being punished. Courts must follow fair procedures rather than act arbitrarily.
Which constitutional protection is described?

choices.A: Due process
choices.B: Eminent domain
choices.C: Executive privilege
choices.D: Direct democracy

questions.correctAnswerText: Due process
questions.explanation: Due process requires fair legal procedures before government can punish a person.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A manufacturing company designs products in one country, buys parts from three other countries, assembles the final product in another country, and sells it worldwide.
Which trend does this example best illustrate?

choices.A: Isolationism
choices.B: Globalization
choices.C: Subsistence farming
choices.D: Constitutional federalism

questions.correctAnswerText: Globalization
questions.explanation: The example shows international production, supply chains, and worldwide markets.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A governor vetoes a bill passed by the state legislature. The legislature then votes again and overrides the veto by the required supermajority.
Which principle is illustrated by this process?

choices.A: Checks and balances
choices.B: Trial by jury
choices.C: Judicial review
choices.D: Naturalization

questions.correctAnswerText: Checks and balances
questions.explanation: A veto and veto override show branches limiting or checking each other’s power.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A city’s population increased from 80,000 to 100,000 over ten years. What was the percent increase?
Type your answer as a whole number percent without the percent sign.
questions.explanation: The increase is 20,000; 20,000 divided by 80,000 equals 25%.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A store sells 500 winter coats in December. In January, the store lowers the price and sells 650 coats.
What is the increase in the number of coats sold?

choices.A: 100 coats
choices.B: 150 coats
choices.C: 500 coats
choices.D: 650 coats

questions.correctAnswerText: 150 coats
questions.explanation: Sales increased from 500 to 650 coats, a difference of 150.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: A person writes a letter to a senator asking for support for a veterans’ benefits bill. This is an example of [D1] and is protected by the right to [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=civic participation; D2=petition the government
questions.explanation: Contacting a senator about legislation is civic participation and is protected by the right to petition.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Paired sources:
Source A:
A New Deal supporter argued that during an economic emergency, the federal government had a responsibility to create jobs, stabilize banks, and provide relief to people who could not find work.
Source B:
A New Deal critic argued that too much federal spending could increase government power and create dependence on public programs.
What was the main disagreement between the two sources?

choices.A: Whether the Great Depression happened
choices.B: Whether the federal government should take a large role in economic relief
choices.C: Whether banks existed before the New Deal
choices.D: Whether workers should be allowed to move between states

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether the federal government should take a large role in economic relief
questions.explanation: Source A supports federal action, while Source B warns against expanded federal power and spending.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A federal court reviews a law passed by Congress and decides that the law violates the Constitution. The court refuses to enforce the law.
Which power is the court using?

choices.A: Legislative veto
choices.B: Judicial review
choices.C: Executive order
choices.D: Popular referendum

questions.correctAnswerText: Judicial review
questions.explanation: Judicial review is the power of courts to decide whether laws violate the Constitution.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

targets.T1: Government authority is divided between national and state governments.
targets.T2: Government leaders and citizens are both subject to the law.
targets.T3: Government power ultimately comes from the people.

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Federalism; T2=Rule of law; T3=Popular sovereignty
questions.explanation: Federalism divides national and state power, rule of law subjects everyone to law, and popular sovereignty means power comes from the people.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A state budget lists the following planned spending.
Program: Education, Transportation, Public Safety, Health Services
Spending: $4.2 billion, $1.6 billion, $1.1 billion, $3.1 billion
Which program receives the largest amount of planned spending?

choices.A: Education
choices.B: Transportation
choices.C: Public Safety
choices.D: Health Services

questions.correctAnswerText: Education
questions.explanation: Education receives $4.2 billion, the largest amount listed.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
The rule of law means that laws must be applied fairly and that government officials cannot act as if they are above the law. Courts, written laws, and public procedures help limit arbitrary power.
Which situation best reflects the rule of law?

choices.A: A mayor ignores traffic laws because of the mayor’s position.
choices.B: A court hears evidence before deciding whether an official broke the law.
choices.C: A legislature cancels elections during a budget debate.
choices.D: A police department punishes a person without a hearing.

questions.correctAnswerText: A court hears evidence before deciding whether an official broke the law.
questions.explanation: The rule of law requires officials to be accountable under fair legal procedures.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
During the Progressive Era, journalists and reformers drew attention to unsafe working conditions, political corruption, and problems in food production. Some reforms led to new regulations, expanded public oversight, and changes in election laws.
Which conclusion is best supported by this source?

choices.A: Progressive reformers sought to address problems caused by industrial society.
choices.B: Progressive reformers wanted to end all federal regulation.
choices.C: The Progressive Era focused only on foreign policy.
choices.D: The Progressive Era eliminated disagreement over government power.

questions.correctAnswerText: Progressive reformers sought to address problems caused by industrial society.
questions.explanation: The source describes reforms responding to unsafe work, corruption, and food production problems.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A farming region depends heavily on one river for irrigation. During a long drought, farms use more groundwater, crop yields fall, and nearby towns debate water restrictions.
Which cause-and-effect relationship is most clearly shown?

choices.A: Water scarcity can affect agriculture and community decisions.
choices.B: Industrialization always reduces the need for water.
choices.C: Migration ends when droughts occur.
choices.D: Rivers have no effect on settlement patterns.

questions.correctAnswerText: Water scarcity can affect agriculture and community decisions.
questions.explanation: The drought reduces crop yields and leads towns to debate water restrictions.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A state legislative committee held hearings on a public safety bill.
Group: Residents, Business owners, Local officials, Advocacy organizations
Number of speakers: 18, 9, 7, 16
How many total speakers testified at the hearings?

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A citizen who is called for jury duty reports to court, listens to instructions, and serves if selected. The citizen does not work for the government but still helps the legal system function.
Which idea does this example best illustrate?

choices.A: Civic responsibility
choices.B: Judicial appointment
choices.C: Executive privilege
choices.D: Interstate commerce

questions.correctAnswerText: Civic responsibility
questions.explanation: Jury duty is a responsibility through which citizens help the justice system operate.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A news article explains that voter registration rules are set by states, but states must still follow federal constitutional protections and federal voting rights laws.
Which TWO statements are supported by this explanation?

choices.A: States have a role in managing elections.
choices.B: States may violate federal voting protections whenever they choose.
choices.C: Federal law can limit state election rules.
choices.D: The Supreme Court counts every ballot directly.
choices.E: Voting rules are unrelated to federalism.

questions.correctAnswerText: States have a role in managing elections. | Federal law can limit state election rules.
questions.explanation: The statement describes election administration as shared between state authority and federal protections.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A town has only enough tax revenue this year to repair roads or build a new public library wing. Officials choose road repairs because several bridges need urgent maintenance.
Which statement best describes the economic reasoning in this decision?

choices.A: Public choices often involve trade-offs because resources are limited.
choices.B: Governments never face scarcity.
choices.C: Tax revenue can be used only for transportation.
choices.D: Opportunity cost applies only to businesses.

questions.correctAnswerText: Public choices often involve trade-offs because resources are limited.
questions.explanation: The town must choose between two uses for limited tax revenue.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A historian compares union membership in one industry.
Year: 1930, 1940, 1950
Union members: 20,000, 85,000, 120,000
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Union membership decreased after 1930.
choices.B: Union membership increased from 1930 to 1950.
choices.C: Union membership stayed the same in each year.
choices.D: Union membership was lower in 1950 than in 1940.

questions.correctAnswerText: Union membership increased from 1930 to 1950.
questions.explanation: Membership rose from 20,000 in 1930 to 120,000 in 1950.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A public official argues, “A free press helps voters learn what government is doing. Reporters may make mistakes, but allowing officials to punish criticism would make it harder for citizens to hold leaders accountable.”
Which claim is best supported by the official’s argument?

choices.A: Freedom of the press supports government accountability.
choices.B: Government officials should approve all newspaper stories before publication.
choices.C: Elections are unnecessary when newspapers exist.
choices.D: The press has no role in a democratic society.

questions.correctAnswerText: Freedom of the press supports government accountability.
questions.explanation: The source argues that a free press helps citizens monitor and hold leaders accountable.
